description_long: This is a vintage, single-propeller biplane with a green and white camouflage paint scheme and black cross insignias on the wings and tail, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The fuselage is slender and elongated, with an open cockpit situated just behind the engine and propeller. The wings are straight and mounted at a low position relative to the fuselage, with no visible dihedral or winglets. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, both marked with black crosses. The landing gear consists of two fixed main wheels and a small tail skid, suitable for grass or dirt runways. The propeller is wooden and mounted at the nose, and the cockpit appears to be configured for a single pilot with minimal instrumentation visible.
description: A vintage green and white camouflaged biplane with black cross insignias, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear. Features a single nose-mounted propeller and conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the spinning propeller, open cockpit, and both main wheels of the fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the black cross insignias on the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the right wing's markings.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the propeller, cockpit, and the right main wheel, with clear visibility of the camouflage pattern.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or winglets; classic biplane structure.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine and propeller.
